what_it_does: Standalone bidirectional sync bridge between a Company fabric channel and an OpenWebUI channel: it polls both sides, mirrors new fabric member posts into OWUI using per-member webhooks (creating them on first sight), and forwards OWUI user-authored messages (treated as Tim) back into fabric, prefixing replies with @member when reply metadata names a webhook user; it persists JSON cursors to avoid floods and echo loops.
observations: Uses environment variables with defaults, including an empty default for OWUI_PASSWORD.
Persists bridge state to a JSON file with atomic write via os.replace.
Creates an OWUI webhook per fabric member on first encounter and caches the id/token.
Skips echo back by tracking bridge_posted_seqs for fabric posts it created and by ignoring OWUI messages that have meta (webhook-origin).
Seeds cursors on first run to latest/max-backfill to avoid flooding both directions.
Re-authenticates to OWUI only when a 401 occurs inside the loop.
Assumes any OWUI message without meta is from Tim and forwards it to fabric.
standouts: Self-registration model: a fabric member's first appearance triggers creation of its own OWUI webhook, baking identity into the webhook.
Reply-aware forwarding: reads reply_to_message.meta.webhook.name (or user.name) to prefix @member in the fabric post.
Loop-suppression via durable state: tracks posted seqs and webhook-origin metadata rather than relying on content signatures.
conventions: Top-level module docstring explains architecture and run modes.
Configuration via os.environ.get with local defaults.
Retry-by-not-marking-seen: failed forwards stay unseen and are retried next pass.
concerns: Default OWUI_PASSWORD is empty and other defaults embed channel IDs and a /home/tim state path.
Webhook tokens are stored in the local JSON state file without encryption.
Loop detection relies on meta being present only for webhook-origin messages; other meta could cause drops or leaks.
Any non-webhook OWUI user message is forwarded as Tim, not just Tim's messages.
No rate limiting, concurrency control, or locking on the state file.
Reply target fallback to rt.user.name may misidentify the intended fabric member.
notes: A stateful, standalone two-way chat bridge that preserves fabric member identity through auto-created OWUI webhooks and suppresses echo with durable JSON cursors.
questions: Is the OWUI webhook creation endpoint path /api/v1/channels/{id}/webhooks/create correct for this OWUI version?
Does the fabric /api/channel/post response reliably contain seq or posted.posted for echo suppression?
Is meta guaranteed to be absent on all human-authored OWUI messages and present only on webhook posts?
How is Tim distinguished from other OWUI human users, given the code forwards any message lacking meta?
What is the exact schema of fabric channel-history posts and OWUI messages?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly aligned: the file does mirror fabric posts via per-member webhooks and forward OWUI replies back to fabric. However, it forwards any user-authored OWUI message as Tim, not strictly messages typed by Tim.
intention_signals: The author is trying to make a fabric room and an OpenWebUI channel behave as one two-way room, preserving who said what and preventing infinite echo, while keeping the solution outside the core systems as a standalone HTTP poller.
novelty: Per-member webhook self-registration on first fabric sighting and the use of reply_to_message webhook metadata to address the original fabric member precisely in replies.
contribution: A reusable pattern for identity-preserving, echo-safe bidirectional channel sync: durable JSON cursors, on-demand webhook provisioning per remote identity, and reply-aware message routing.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone Python bridge that polls a Company fabric channel and an OpenWebUI channel to mirror fabric member posts into OWUI via per-member webhooks and forward OWUI user messages back to fabric, tracking durable JSON cursors to prevent echo and flood.
intention_for_embedding: To enable two-way communication between a fabric channel and an OpenWebUI channel while preserving fabric member identity and avoiding message loops.

symbols.does: Reads the durable JSON state file or returns an empty dict if it is missing/unreadable.
symbols.description: Loads persisted bridge cursors and webhook cache from STATE; swallows all exceptions and starts fresh, which is safe but masks corruption.

symbols.does: Atomically writes the bridge state dictionary to the JSON state file.
symbols.description: Creates the parent directory if needed, writes to a temporary file, then replaces the original with os.replace to reduce the risk of a torn write.

symbols.does: Authenticates to OpenWebUI with email/password and returns the bearer token.
symbols.description: Fails fast if OWUI_PASSWORD is unset; posts to /api/v1/auths/signin and returns the token from the response.

symbols.does: Creates and caches an OWUI webhook for a fabric member on first sight.
symbols.description: Self-registration helper: checks the state cache, and if absent creates a webhook named after the member in the OWUI channel, storing its id and token for later posts.

symbols.does: Mirrors new fabric posts into the OWUI channel using each member's own webhook.
symbols.description: Polls fabric channel-history, seeds the cursor to the latest seq on first run, then posts each newer non-bridge message to OWUI via ensure_member_webhook, advancing fabric_last_seq and skipping seqs the bridge itself created.

symbols.does: Forwards OWUI user-authored messages to the fabric channel, prefixing replies with @member.
symbols.description: Polls OWUI messages oldest-first, ignores webhook-origin messages and already-seen ids, prefixes replies when reply_to_message names a webhook/user, posts to fabric, and records the returned seq for echo suppression; failures are retried because ids stay unseen.

symbols.does: Runs one fabric-to-OWUI and one OWUI-to-fabric sync cycle and saves state.
symbols.description: Orchestrates a single bidirectional pass, persists the updated cursors, and returns the counts mirrored and forwarded.

symbols.does: CLI entry point that signs in, loads state, and runs either one pass or a polling loop.
symbols.description: Parses --once/--loop/--interval arguments, prints configuration, obtains an OWUI token, and drives continuous sync with re-authentication on 401.
